clauses.detail: Non-exclusive right to operate home inspection franchise within specified territory. Territory defined by map attached as Exhibit A to franchise agreement. Must maintain all docs/records at Approved Location in franchisee's state.

5-year initial term. Up to 5 renewal terms of 5 years each. Renewal conditions: 6 months written notice, full compliance, current on payments, $2,500 fee, sign then-current franchise agreement (may be materially different), sign general release (Exhibit G-4). Territory may be modified at renewal. Fees on renewal capped at similarly-situated renewing franchisees.

Initial fee $58,500. Royalty 7% with escalating minimums ($0-$2,328/mo). Brand Fee 4%. IT Fee $5.50/inspection. PTP360 $3.10/inspection (agreement says $3.00). PTPFloorplan $21-$350. PTPVirtualOpenHouse $10. PTPHomeManual $9.45. EZBook $33.45/$29.75. Financial Reporting $30/mo. Brand Conference $1,099 (payable in 12 installments). All fees adjustable 1x/year on 90 days notice. 18% interest on late payments. No set-off right. EFT required.
Must open within 120 days of training completion. Must obtain all licenses/permits. Must order initial inventory 30 days before opening. Time is of the essence.

Must complete Initial Training Program to franchisor's sole subjective satisfaction. All inspectors must complete training at franchisee's expense. Mandatory annual Brand Conference and one regional meeting. Mandatory continuing education. Must maintain professional association membership.

Strict conformity with all mandatory policies. Must sell only approved products/services. Must offer Plus/Premium/Prestige packages. No self-referral for repairs. Minimum revenue requirements ($100K-$500K escalating). Default if missed 1 year; termination if missed 2 consecutive years (Section 15.2.10). Must permit inspections. Must implement all system changes at own expense. Must maintain minimum staffing. No unilateral system modifications.

Non-exclusive license. All goodwill inures to franchisor. No ownership interest in marks. Must use only as authorized. Franchisor may substitute different marks (franchisor bears signage cost). Domain names and social media accounts irrevocably assigned to franchisor.

Must operate per Manual. Manual is franchisor property. Must use Technology Platform. No limitation on upgrade frequency or cost. Must maintain 2 business telephone lines (franchisor owns/controls). Must implement all data privacy safeguards. Must comply with AI policies — prohibited from entering proprietary data into public AI models.

Perpetual obligation — during and after term. Customer data is franchisor's trade secret. No disclosure to AI models without written authorization. All staff with access must sign confidentiality covenants.

GAAP books preserved 7 years. Monthly revenue/inspection reports by 5th of month. Annual reviewed financial statements within 90 days of fiscal year end. Audit rights at all times during business hours. Audit cost to franchisee if >2% understatement.

4% Brand Fee to Brand Fund. Fund administered at franchisor's sole discretion. No obligation to spend proportionately in franchisee's territory. All advertising requires prior written approval (30 days). No internet/social media presence without written approval. Franchisor may withhold for any or no reason.

Required types and amounts per Manual. Must name franchisor and FS Brands as additional insureds. Primary coverage. 30-day cancellation notice. Certificates required before opening and 15 days before expiration.

Franchisor may freely assign. Franchisee transfer requires written approval. Conditions: transferee qualifies, completes training, $4,000 fee, $15,000 referral fee if applicable, all defaults cured, transferee signs new agreement (may be different, expires on original date), general release required, 2-year extended E&O insurance. Entity transfer to wholly-owned entity: $1,000 fee (waived if within 6 months). Franchisor right of first refusal to match any offer within 30 days. Death/disability: estate has 120 days.

Automatic without notice: insolvency, bankruptcy, assignment for creditors, receivership, dissolution. By notice without cure: failure to complete training, failure to open, abandonment, conviction, public safety threat, unauthorized transfer, failure to meet minimum revenue 2 consecutive years, confidentiality breach, 3+ notices in 12 months, failure to implement software, failure to attend conferences. Curable: 10 days monetary, 30 days other.

Must pay all amounts owed. Cease using marks, confidential info, Manual. Cancel assumed name registrations. Assign telephone numbers and social media to franchisor. Procure 2-year extended E&O coverage naming franchisor as additional insured. Return all franchisor property.

During term: no interest in competing business. Post-term: 2 years within territory. All principals, managers, employees with access must sign Exhibit G-5 confidentiality/non-competition agreement. Subject to state law.

Franchisee indemnifies and holds harmless franchisor from all claims arising from franchisee's operation, breaches, unauthorized acts. Franchisor indemnifies franchisee for claims arising from franchisor's breach of agreement.

Except for injunctive relief and certain claims, all disputes submitted to binding arbitration in Tampa, Florida. AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules. Single arbitrator. Florida law governs. Waiver of punitive/exemplary damages. Waiver of class action. 1-year limitation period (or shorter if permitted by law). Each party bears own costs. Subject to state law overrides.

distinctive_clauses.detail: Expressly prohibits entering customer data or proprietary information into public/open AI models. Requires written authorization for any AI model training use.
Irrevocable assignment to franchisor of all domain names and social media accounts used in franchise, whether or not approved.
Performance plan required if minimum revenue missed once. Termination if missed 2 consecutive years. Minimums escalate from $100K to $500K.
Waiver of right to punitive or exemplary damages. May not be enforceable in California and other states.
Claims must be brought within 1 year. May not be enforceable in states requiring longer periods.
Co-opetition rules for franchisees in shared territories. Must coordinate marketing, share opportunities, communicate regularly.
Cross-territory referral rules. May enter neighbor's territory only if specifically asked by a referral source from your territory.

guaranty_scope.obligations: All covenants, payments, agreements, undertakings, and obligations under franchise agreement
guaranty_scope.waivers: Right to require franchisor to proceed against franchisee first, exhaust security, or pursue any remedy
guaranty_scope.who_must_sign: All shareholders (corp), all members (LLC), all partners (partnership)
